name: DVC
tagline: Git-like version control for data, models, and ML experiments
seoTitle: DVC - Data & ML Experiment Version Control
metaDescription: DVC is a command-line tool that versions datasets, models, and ML experiments alongside Git, so machine learning projects stay reproducible and shareable.
overview: DVC (Data Version Control) is a command-line tool and VS Code extension for building reproducible machine learning projects. It lets you version data and models by keeping the large files in cloud or network storage while tracking their version info inside your Git repository.

It is built for ML engineers and data science teams who already use Git for code but need a way to track datasets, model files, pipelines, and experiments. Small placeholder meta-files live in Git, and DVC handles moving the actual data to and from a cache and remote storage.

As an experiment-tracking tool, DVC works locally in your existing Git repo with no separate server. You can define pipelines that rerun only the affected steps, run and compare many experiments by their parameters and metrics, and reproduce anyone's run from the same Git history.
features: Version datasets and model files in cloud or on-prem storage while keeping version info in Git
Define lightweight ML pipelines that rerun only the steps affected by your changes
Track experiments locally in your Git repo with no extra servers
Compare data, code, parameters, models, and performance plots across runs
Push and pull data to remote storage such as S3, Azure, Google Cloud, or SSH
Optional VS Code extension to drive DVC from a GUI inside the IDE

gettingStarted.intro: Install the DVC CLI, initialize it inside an existing Git repository, then start tracking data and running experiments.

steps.heading: Install DVC
steps.body: Install the CLI from PyPI with pip. DVC is also available via Homebrew, Conda, Chocolatey, and Snap.

steps.heading: Track a dataset
steps.body: Use git for code and dvc add for data. DVC stores the data in its cache and creates a small meta-file you commit to Git.
steps.code:
git add train.py params.yaml
dvc add images/

steps.heading: Define pipeline stages
steps.body: Connect code and data into a pipeline so DVC knows how outputs are produced from inputs and commands.
steps.code:
dvc stage add -n featurize -d images/ -o features/ python featurize.py
dvc stage add -n train -d features/ -d train.py -o model.p -M metrics.json python train.py

steps.heading: Run and compare experiments
steps.body: Run experiments, then list and apply the one you want. Push data and models to a remote to share them.
steps.code:
dvc exp run -n exp-baseline
dvc exp show
dvc remote add myremote -d s3://mybucket/image_cnn
dvc push

useCases: Version large training datasets and model files alongside code without bloating the Git repo
Build reproducible ML pipelines that rerun only the steps impacted by a change
Track and compare many experiments by hyperparameters and metrics in a local Git repo
Share data and models with a team via S3, Azure, Google Cloud, or SSH remote storage
